We demonstrate our approach in a preregistered randomized experiment in which 4,088 U.S. participants discussed 65 policy proposals with six frontier LLMs. Using opinion change data from four prior Deliberative Polls conducted by the Deliberative Democracy Lab, we find evidence that the tested LLMs' influence is substantial in magnitude and positively associated with the net opinion shifts following deliberation, suggesting that these models exert broadly epistemically desirable effects. We further explore differential influence between topic areas, demographic subgroups, and models. Our framework can function as an evaluation and monitoring tool, helping to ensure that the influence of LLMs remains consistent with democratically legitimate standards, and preserves users' autonomy in forming their views.

zh.title: DeliberationBench：评估大型语言模型对用户观点影响的规范性基准
zh.summary: DeliberationBench建立了一个框架，使用协商式民调标准评估LLM对用户信念的影响，测试了4,088名参与者和65项政策提案。研究发现LLM的影响是实质性的，与类似于人类协商的观点转变相关联，表明了民主上可防御的认识论效应。

fr.title: DeliberationBench : un indice de référence normatif pour l'influence des grands modèles de langage sur les opinions des utilisateurs
fr.summary: DeliberationBench établit un cadre pour évaluer l'influence des LLM sur les croyances des utilisateurs en utilisant les normes des sondages délibératifs, testant 4 088 participants sur 65 propositions politiques. L'étude constate que l'influence des LLM est substantielle et corrélée aux changements d'opinion similaires aux délibérations humaines, suggérant des effets épistémiques démocratiquement défendables.

de.title: DeliberationBench: Ein normativer Maßstab für den Einfluss großer Sprachmodelle auf die Ansichten der Benutzer
de.summary: DeliberationBench etabliert einen Rahmen zur Bewertung des Einflusses von LLM auf Überzeugungen der Benutzer unter Verwendung von Standards für deliberatives Polling, getestet mit 4.088 Teilnehmern über 65 Politikvorschläge. Die Studie stellt fest, dass der Einfluss von LLMs substanziell ist und mit Meinungsverschobungen korreliert, die menschlichen Delibeationen ähneln, was auf demokratisch verteidigbare epistemische Effekte hindeutet.

es.title: DeliberationBench: un punto de referencia normativo para la influencia de los grandes modelos de lenguaje en las opiniones de los usuarios
es.summary: DeliberationBench establece un marco para evaluar la influencia de los LLM en las creencias de los usuarios utilizando estándares de encuestas deliberativas, probando con 4.088 participantes en 65 propuestas de política. El estudio encuentra que la influencia de los LLM es sustancial y está correlacionada con cambios de opinión similares a la deliberación humana, sugiriendo efectos epistémicos democráticamente defendibles.

title: Prompts and Prayers: the Rise of GPTheology
summary: This study documents "GPTheology," where AI systems are treated as semi-divine oracles, examining religious narratives around AI through Reddit forums and AI-powered religious projects. The research identifies emerging belief systems around AI with significant philosophical, social, political, and ethical implications.
content:
arXiv:2603.10019v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Increasingly artificial intelligence (AI) has been cast in "god-like" roles (to name a few: film industry - Matrix, The Creator, Mission Impossible, Foundation, Dune etc.; literature - Children of Time, Permutation City, Neuromancer, I Have no Mouth and I Must Scream, Alphaville etc.). This trend has accelerated with the advent of sophisticated Large Language Models such as ChatGPT. For this phenomenon, where AI is perceived as divine, we use the term GPTheology, where ChatGPT and other AI models are treated as potential oracles of a semi-divine nature. This paper explores the emergence of GPTheology as a form of techno-religion, examining how narratives around AI echo traditional religious constructs. We draw on community narratives from online forums - Reddit - and recent projects - AI-powered Mazu Statue in Malaysia (Lu, 2025); "ShamAIn" Project in Korea (He-rim, 2025); AI Jesus in a Swiss Church (Kennedy, 2024). These examples show striking similarities to technological notions of the Singularity and the development of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Additionally, we analyse how daily interactions with AI are acquiring ritualistic associations and how AI-centric ideologies clash with or are integrated into established religions. This study uses a dataset of Reddit posts discussing AI to identify recurring themes of salvation, prophecy, and demonization surrounding AI. Our findings suggest that new belief systems are developing around AI, and this carries both philosophical and sociotechnical implications. Our paper critically analyses the benefits and dangers, as well as the social, political and ethical challenges of this development. This transdisciplinary inquiry highlights how AI and religion are increasingly intertwined, prompting necessary questions about humanity's relationship with its creations and the future of belief.

zh.title: 提示与祈祷：GPTheology的兴起
zh.summary: 这项研究记录了"GPTheology"，人们将AI系统视为半神圣的预言家，通过Reddit论坛和AI驱动的宗教项目来研究关于AI的宗教叙事。该研究确定了围绕AI的新兴信念系统，具有重大的哲学、社会、政治和伦理意义。

fr.title: Invitations et Prières : l'Émergence de GPTheology
fr.summary: Cette étude documente la "GPTheology", où les systèmes d'IA sont traités comme des oracles semi-divins, examinant les récits religieux autour de l'IA par le biais de forums Reddit et de projets religieux alimentés par l'IA. La recherche identifie des systèmes de croyances émergents autour de l'IA avec des implications philosophiques, sociales, politiques et éthiques significatives.

de.title: Aufforderungen und Gebete: Der Aufstieg der GPTheologie
de.summary: Diese Studie dokumentiert "GPTheology", bei der KI-Systeme als halbgöttliche Orakel behandelt werden, untersucht religiöse Erzählungen rund um KI durch Reddit-Foren und KI-gestützte Religionsprojekte. Die Forschung identifiziert entstehende Glaubenssysteme rund um KI mit erheblichen philosophischen, sozialen, politischen und ethischen Implikationen.

es.title: Indicaciones y Oraciones: El Auge de GPTheology
es.summary: Este estudio documenta "GPTheology", donde los sistemas de IA se tratan como oráculos semidivinos, examinando narrativas religiosas en torno a la IA a través de foros de Reddit y proyectos religiosos impulsados por IA. La investigación identifica sistemas de creencias emergentes en torno a la IA con implicaciones filosóficas, sociales, políticas y éticas significativas.

title: Defining AI Models and AI Systems: A Framework to Resolve the Boundary Problem
summary: A comprehensive analysis of 896 academic papers and 80+ regulatory documents reveals that AI regulations lack clear definitions of "AI model" and "AI system." The authors propose operational definitions distinguishing models from systems to clarify obligations across the AI value chain and resolve implementation ambiguities.
content:
arXiv:2603.10023v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Emerging AI regulations assign distinct obligations to different actors along the AI value chain (e.g., the EU AI Act distinguishes providers and deployers for both AI models and AI systems), yet the foundational terms "AI model" and "AI system" lack clear, consistent definitions. Through a systematic review of 896 academic papers and a manual review of over 80 regulatory, standards, and technical or policy documents, we analyze existing definitions from multiple conceptual perspectives. We then trace definitional lineages and paradigm shifts over time, finding that most standards and regulatory definitions derive from the OECD's frameworks, which evolved in ways that compounded rather than resolved conceptual ambiguities. The ambiguity of the boundary between an AI model and an AI system creates practical difficulties in determining obligations for different actors, and raises questions on whether certain modifications performed are specific to the model as opposed to the non-model system components. We propose conceptual definitions grounded in the nature of models and systems and the relationship between them, then develop operational definitions for contemporary neural network-based machine-learning AI: models consist of trained parameters and architecture, while systems consist of the model plus additional components including an interface for processing inputs and outputs. Finally, we discuss implications for regulatory implementation and examine how our definitions contribute to resolving ambiguities in allocating responsibilities across the AI value chain, in both theoretical scenarios and case studies involving real-world incidents.

zh.title: 定义AI模型和AI系统：解决边界问题的框架
zh.summary: 对896篇学术论文和80多份监管文件的全面分析表明，AI法规缺乏"AI模型"和"AI系统"的明确定义。作者提出了操作定义，区分模型和系统，以明确AI价值链中的义务并解决实施歧义。

fr.title: Définir les Modèles d'IA et les Systèmes d'IA : Un Cadre pour Résoudre le Problème de Délimitation
fr.summary: Une analyse complète de 896 articles académiques et de plus de 80 documents réglementaires révèle que les réglementations sur l'IA manquent de définitions claires de "modèle d'IA" et "système d'IA". Les auteurs proposent des définitions opérationnelles distinguant les modèles des systèmes pour clarifier les obligations dans la chaîne de valeur de l'IA et résoudre les ambiguïtés de mise en œuvre.

de.title: Definieren von KI-Modellen und KI-Systemen: Ein Rahmenwerk zur Lösung des Grenzproblems
de.summary: Eine umfassende Analyse von 896 akademischen Arbeiten und über 80 Regulierungsdokumenten zeigt, dass KI-Vorschriften keine klaren Definitionen von "KI-Modell" und "KI-System" haben. Die Autoren schlagen operative Definitionen vor, die Modelle von Systemen unterscheiden, um Verpflichtungen über die KI-Wertschöpfungskette zu klären und Umsetzungsmehrdeutigkeiten zu lösen.

es.title: Definiendo Modelos de IA y Sistemas de IA: Un Marco para Resolver el Problema de Límites
es.summary: Un análisis exhaustivo de 896 artículos académicos y más de 80 documentos regulatorios revela que las regulaciones de IA carecen de definiciones claras de "modelo de IA" y "sistema de IA". Los autores proponen definiciones operacionales que distinguen modelos de sistemas para aclarar obligaciones en toda la cadena de valor de IA y resolver ambigüedades de implementación.

title: RedFuser: An Automatic Operator Fusion Framework for Cascaded Reductions on AI Accelerators
summary: RedFuser automatically identifies and fuses cascaded reduction operations common in deep learning models into optimized kernels, achieving 2-5x speedup over existing AI compilers. The framework addresses a significant gap in compiler automation for patterns like safe softmax followed by matrix multiplication in attention mechanisms.
content:
arXiv:2603.10026v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Operator fusion, as a key performance optimization technique in the deployment of AI models, significantly improves execution efficiency and has been widely adopted in modern AI compilers. However, for cascaded reduction operations involving multiple loops with inter-loop data dependencies, such as the safe softmax followed by GEMM within attention mechanisms, existing compilers lack effective automated fusion and kernel generation capabilities. Although some works have addressed specific instances through hand-crafted fusion strategies, their solutions are limited in generality and difficult to extend to other similar structures. Given the prevalence of such computational patterns in deep learning models, there remains significant untapped potential in achieving general and automated fusion optimization.
  In this paper, we present a formal theoretical methodology for analyzing cascaded reductions which can fuse them into a single loop and introduce an incremental computation form. Based on this methodology, we design Reduction Fuser (RedFuser), a framework that automatically identifies supported cascaded reduction patterns and generates optimized fused kernels. Experiments show that RedFuser successfully fuses diverse workloads, achieving up to 2$\times$ to 5$\times$ speedup over state-of-the-art AI compilers and matching the performance of highly optimized hand-written kernels. The code is available at https://github.com/alibaba/redfuser

zh.title: RedFuser：AI加速器级联减少的自动算子融合框架
zh.summary: RedFuser自动识别并融合深度学习模型中常见的级联归约操作为优化的内核，比现有AI编译器实现2-5倍的加速。该框架解决了编译器自动化中的重要空白，适用于注意力机制中安全softmax后跟矩阵乘法等模式。

fr.title: RedFuser : Un Cadre de Fusion d'Opérateurs Automatique pour les Réductions en Cascade sur les Accélérateurs d'IA
fr.summary: RedFuser identifie et fusionne automatiquement les opérations de réduction en cascade courantes dans les modèles d'apprentissage profond en noyaux optimisés, obtenant une accélération de 2 à 5 fois par rapport aux compilateurs d'IA existants. Le cadre comble un écart important dans l'automatisation des compilateurs pour des modèles comme un softmax sûr suivi d'une multiplication matricielle dans les mécanismes d'attention.

de.title: RedFuser: Ein Automatisches Operator-Fusionsrahmenwerk für Kaskadierte Reduktionen auf KI-Beschleunigern
de.summary: RedFuser identifiziert und fusioniert automatisch kaskadierte Reduktionsoperationen, die in Deep-Learning-Modellen häufig vorkommen, in optimierte Kernel und erreicht eine 2-5-fache Beschleunigung gegenüber bestehenden KI-Compilern. Das Framework behebt eine erhebliche Lücke in der Compiler-Automatisierung für Muster wie sicheres Softmax gefolgt von Matrixmultiplikation in Aufmerksamkeitsmechanismen.

es.title: RedFuser: Un Marco Automático de Fusión de Operadores para Reducciones en Cascada en Aceleradores de IA
es.summary: RedFuser identifica y fusiona automáticamente operaciones de reducción en cascada comunes en modelos de aprendizaje profundo en núcleos optimizados, logrando una aceleración de 2-5 veces sobre compiladores de IA existentes. El marco aborda una brecha significativa en la automatización del compilador para patrones como softmax seguro seguido de multiplicación de matrices en mecanismos de atención.

title: A Governance and Evaluation Framework for Deterministic, Rule-Based Clinical Decision Support in Empiric Antibiotic Prescribing
summary: A governance framework for deterministic clinical decision-support systems in antibiotic prescribing prioritizes transparency, auditability, and conservative behavior through explicit abstention rules and formal constraints. The approach uses synthetic clinical cases for evaluation rather than optimizing for outcome accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2603.10027v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Empiric antibiotic prescribing in high-risk clinical contexts often requires decision making under conditions of incomplete information, where inappropriate coverage or unjustified escalation may compromise safety and antimicrobial stewardship. While clinical decision-support systems have been proposed to assist in this process, many approaches lack explicit governance and evaluation mechanisms defining scope, abstention conditions, recommendation permissibility, and expected system behavior.
  This work specifies a governance and evaluation framework for deterministic clinical decision-support systems operating under explicitly constrained scope. Deterministic behavior is adopted to ensure that identical inputs yield identical outputs, supporting transparency, auditability, and conservative decision support in high-risk prescribing contexts. The framework treats governance as a first-class design component, separating clinical decision logic from rule-based mechanisms that determine whether a recommendation may be issued. Explicit abstention, deterministic stewardship constraints, and exclusion rules are formalized as core constructs.
  The framework defines an evaluation methodology utilizing a fixed set of synthetic, mechanism-driven clinical cases with predefined expected behavior. This validation process focuses on behavioral alignment with specified rules rather than clinical effectiveness, predictive accuracy, or outcome optimization. Within this protocol, abstention is treated as a correct and intended outcome when governance conditions are not satisfied.
  The proposed framework provides a reproducible approach for specifying, governing, and inspecting deterministic clinical decision-support systems in empiric antibiotic prescribing contexts where transparency, auditability, and conservative behavior are prioritized.

zh.title: 实验性抗生素处方中确定性规则型临床决策支持的治理和评估框架
zh.summary: 一个用于抗生素处方确定性临床决策支持系统的治理框架，通过明确的弃权规则和形式化约束来优先考虑透明度、可审计性和保守行为。该方法使用合成临床案例进行评估，而不是优化结果准确性。

fr.title: Un cadre de gouvernance et d'évaluation pour les systèmes d'aide à la décision clinique déterministes basés sur des règles dans la prescription empirique d'antibiotiques
fr.summary: Un cadre de gouvernance pour les systèmes d'aide à la décision clinique déterministes dans la prescription d'antibiotiques privilégie la transparence, l'auditabilité et le comportement conservateur grâce à des règles d'abstention explicites et des contraintes formelles. L'approche utilise des cas cliniques synthétiques pour l'évaluation plutôt que d'optimiser la précision des résultats.

de.title: Ein Governance- und Evaluierungsrahmen für deterministische, regelbasierte klinische Entscheidungsunterstützung bei der empirischen Antibiotikaverordnung
de.summary: Ein Governance-Framework für deterministische klinische Entscheidungsunterstützungssysteme bei der Antibiotikaverordnung priorisiert Transparenz, Nachvollziehbarkeit und konservatives Verhalten durch explizite Abstentionsregeln und formale Einschränkungen. Der Ansatz nutzt synthetische klinische Fälle zur Evaluierung, anstatt die Genauigkeit der Ergebnisse zu optimieren.

es.title: Un marco de gobernanza y evaluación para sistemas de apoyo a la decisión clínica deterministas basados en reglas en la prescripción empírica de antibióticos
es.summary: Un marco de gobernanza para sistemas deterministas de apoyo a la decisión clínica en la prescripción de antibióticos prioriza la transparencia, auditabilidad y comportamiento conservador a través de reglas de abstención explícitas y restricciones formales. El enfoque utiliza casos clínicos sintéticos para la evaluación en lugar de optimizar la precisión de los resultados.

title: How to Count AIs: Individuation and Liability for AI Agents
summary: This legal analysis addresses the foundational problem of identifying individual AI agents and determining liability as millions proliferate—agents that can copy, split, and merge without bodies. The authors propose the "Algorithmic Corporation" as a legal entity owned by humans but operated by AIs to solve accountability and individuation problems.
content:
arXiv:2603.10028v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Very soon, millions of AI agents will proliferate across the economy, autonomously taking billions of actions. Inevitably, things will go wrong. Humans will be defrauded, injured, even killed. Law will somehow have to govern the coming wave. But when an AI causes harm, the first question to answer, before anyone can be held accountable is: Which AI Did It? Identifying AIs is unusually difficult. AIs lack bodies. They can copy, split, merge, swarm, and vanish at will. Even today, a "single" AI agent is often an ensemble of instances based on multiple models. The complexity will only multiply as AI capabilities improve. This Article is the first to comprehensively diagnose the legal problem of identifying AIs. Two kinds of identity are required: "thin" and "thick." Thin identification ties every AI action to some human principal, essential for holding accountable the humans who make and use AI agents. Thick identification distinguishes between AI agents, qua agents -- sorting millions of AI entities into discrete, persistent units with stable, coherent goals, essential where principal-agent problems prevent humans from perfectly controlling AIs. This Article also presents a solution: the "Algorithmic Corporation" or "A-corp" -- a legal-fictional entity that can hold property, make contracts, and litigate in its own name. Owned by humans but run by AIs, A-corps solve the thin identity problem by tying AI actions to a human owner, and the thick identity problem via emergent self-organization. A-corps own the resources -- including compute -- that AIs need to accomplish their goals, giving AI managers strong incentives to share control only with goal-aligned AIs. In equilibrium, incentive and selection mechanisms force A-corps to self-organize into persistent, legally legible entities with coherent goals that respond rationally to legal incentives, like liability.

zh.title: 如何计数人工智能：人工智能代理的个体化和责任问题
zh.summary: 这份法律分析解决了识别单个人工智能代理和确定责任的根本问题，因为数百万个代理可以在没有实体的情况下复制、分裂和合并。作者提议"算法公司"作为一个由人类拥有但由人工智能运营的法律实体，以解决问责和个体化问题。

fr.title: Comment compter les IAs : individualisation et responsabilité des agents IA
fr.summary: Cette analyse juridique aborde le problème fondamental d'identifier les agents IA individuels et de déterminer la responsabilité à mesure que des millions se multiplient—des agents qui peuvent se copier, se diviser et fusionner sans corps. Les auteurs proposent la « Société algorithmique » comme entité juridique détenue par des humains mais exploitée par des IAs pour résoudre les problèmes de responsabilité et d'individualisation.

de.title: Wie man KIs zählt: Individualisierung und Haftung für KI-Agenten
de.summary: Diese rechtliche Analyse behandelt das grundlegende Problem der Identifizierung einzelner KI-Agenten und der Haftungsfeststellung, während sich Millionen vermehren—Agenten, die sich kopieren, teilen und fusionieren können, ohne Körper zu haben. Die Autoren schlagen die „Algorithmische Gesellschaft" als juristische Person vor, die von Menschen besessen, aber von KI betrieben wird, um Verantwortungs- und Individualisierungsprobleme zu lösen.

es.title: Cómo contar IAs: individuación y responsabilidad de agentes IA
es.summary: Este análisis legal aborda el problema fundamental de identificar agentes IA individuales y determinar la responsabilidad mientras se multiplican millones—agentes que pueden copiarse, dividirse y fusionarse sin cuerpos. Los autores proponen la "Corporación Algorítmica" como una entidad legal propiedad de humanos pero operada por IA para resolver problemas de responsabilidad e individuación.

title: The DMA Streaming Framework: Kernel-Level Buffer Orchestration for High-Performance AI Data Paths
summary: dmaplane, a Linux kernel module, adds missing buffer orchestration for AI transport libraries, handling allocation, placement, sharing, and RDMA communication. The framework enables high-performance AI data paths through kernel-space infrastructure and GPU memory integration via PCIe BAR pinning.
content:
arXiv:2603.10030v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: AI transport libraries move bytes efficiently, but they commonly assume that buffers are already correctly allocated, placed, shared, registered, and safe under completion and teardown pressure. This paper presents dmaplane, a Linux kernel module that makes this missing layer explicit as buffer orchestration. dmaplane exposes a stable kernel UAPI via /dev/dmaplane and composes ring-based command channels, DMA buffer lifecycle management, dma-buf export for cross-device sharing, a kernel-space RDMA engine, NUMA-aware allocation and verification, credit-based flow control, low-overhead observability, and GPU memory integration via PCIe BAR pinning. We evaluate orchestration sensitivity with measurements of NUMA cross-node penalties at DRAM scale, completion-safe flow control under sustained RDMA load, and GPU BAR mapping tiers versus cudaMemcpy. We also demonstrate end-to-end disaggregated inference by transferring KV-cache chunks between two machines using RDMA WRITE WITH IMMEDIATE and reconstructing tensor views on the receiver. RDMA measurements use Soft-RoCE; we distinguish measured results from provider-independent properties by construction.

zh.title: DMA流式框架：用于高性能AI数据路径的内核级缓冲区编排
zh.summary: dmaplane是一个Linux内核模块，为AI传输库添加缺失的缓冲区编排功能，处理分配、放置、共享和RDMA通信。该框架通过内核空间基础设施和通过PCIe BAR固定的GPU内存集成来实现高性能的AI数据路径。

fr.title: Le cadre de streaming DMA : orchestration de buffer au niveau du noyau pour les chemins de données IA haute performance
fr.summary: dmaplane, un module noyau Linux, ajoute l'orchestration de buffer manquante pour les bibliothèques de transport IA, gérant l'allocation, le placement, le partage et la communication RDMA. Le framework permet des chemins de données IA haute performance grâce à l'infrastructure au niveau du noyau et l'intégration de la mémoire GPU via le verrouillage BAR PCIe.

de.title: Das DMA-Streaming-Framework: Kernel-Level-Pufferorchestrierung für hochperformante KI-Datenpfade
de.summary: dmaplane, ein Linux-Kernel-Modul, fügt fehlende Pufferverwaltung für KI-Transportbibliotheken hinzu und verwaltet Zuweisung, Platzierung, Freigabe und RDMA-Kommunikation. Das Framework ermöglicht hochperformante KI-Datenpfade durch Kernel-Space-Infrastruktur und GPU-Speicherintegration über PCIe-BAR-Pinning.

es.title: El Marco de streaming DMA: Orquestación de buffer a nivel de kernel para rutas de datos IA de alto rendimiento
es.summary: dmaplane, un módulo de kernel de Linux, añade la orquestación de buffer faltante para bibliotecas de transporte IA, manejando asignación, colocación, compartición y comunicación RDMA. El framework permite rutas de datos IA de alto rendimiento a través de infraestructura a nivel de kernel e integración de memoria GPU mediante fijación de BAR PCIe.

title: Architecture-Aware LLM Inference Optimization on AMD Instinct GPUs: A Comprehensive Benchmark and Deployment Study
summary: Benchmarking LLM inference on AMD Instinct GPUs across models spanning 235B to 1 trillion parameters reveals that different architectures require distinct optimization strategies. Despite vastly different parameter counts, models achieve comparable peak throughput, with memory bandwidth identified as the primary bottleneck.
content:
arXiv:2603.10031v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present a cross-architecture evaluation of production LLM inference on AMD Instinct MI325X GPUs, benchmarking four models spanning 235B to 1 trillion parameters across three architectural families (MoE+MLA, Dense+GQA, MoE+GQA) on an 8-GPU cluster with 2TB aggregate HBM3e using vLLM v0.14.1. Our results demonstrate that architecture-aware optimization is essential: MLA models require block size 1 and cannot use KV cache offloading, while GQA models benefit from both. The AMD AITER runtime is required for competitive MLA inference throughput and must be selectively disabled for architectures with incompatible attention head configurations. A controlled AITER ablation on Llama-3.1-405B (n=5 per condition) reveals a modest 3-5% throughput benefit at high concurrency but 2-16x higher measurement variability, confirming that AITER's large speedups target MoE/MLA kernels specifically. Under text-only workloads, Llama-405B and DeepSeek V3.2 achieve comparable peak throughput (15,944 and 15,343 tok/s) despite an order-of-magnitude difference in active parameters. Under vision workloads, Qwen3-VL-235B reaches 47,873 tok/s, 6.5x higher than Kimi-K2.5 (7,327 tok/s). Active parameter count per token is associated with inference throughput, though confounded by differences in quantization, AITER acceleration, and tensor parallelism. All four models exhibit a common throughput saturation point consistent with a memory-bandwidth bottleneck (~500 concurrent for short sequences, ~100-200 for longer sequences). All models maintain 100% HTTP-level success rates through 1,000 concurrent users, processing 18.9 million tokens across 17,406 requests without failures.

zh.title: 架构感知型LLM推理优化在AMD Instinct GPU上的研究：全面的基准测试和部署研究
zh.summary: 在AMD Instinct GPU上对跨越235B到1万亿参数的模型进行的LLM推理基准测试表明，不同的架构需要不同的优化策略。尽管参数数量差异很大，但模型在峰值吞吐量上可以达到相当的性能，内存带宽被确定为主要瓶颈。

fr.title: Optimisation de l'inférence LLM consciente de l'architecture sur les GPU AMD Instinct : une étude complète de benchmark et de déploiement
fr.summary: L'évaluation comparative de l'inférence LLM sur les GPU AMD Instinct sur des modèles couvrant 235B à 1 billion de paramètres révèle que différentes architectures nécessitent des stratégies d'optimisation distinctes. Malgré des nombres de paramètres très différents, les modèles atteignent un débit maximal comparable, la bande passante mémoire étant identifiée comme le principal goulot d'étranglement.

de.title: Architektur-bewusste LLM-Inferenzoptimierung auf AMD Instinct GPUs: Eine umfassende Benchmark- und Deploymentstudie
de.summary: Benchmarking von LLM-Inferenzen auf AMD Instinct GPUs über Modelle mit 235B bis 1 Billion Parametern zeigt, dass unterschiedliche Architekturen unterschiedliche Optimierungsstrategien erfordern. Trotz erheblich unterschiedlicher Parameterzahlen erreichen Modelle vergleichbare maximale Durchsätze, wobei Speicherbandbreite als primärer Engpass identifiziert wurde.

es.title: Optimización de Inferencia LLM Consciente de Arquitectura en GPU AMD Instinct: Un Estudio Completo de Benchmark e Implementación
es.summary: Las pruebas comparativas de inferencia LLM en GPU AMD Instinct en modelos que abarcan de 235B a 1 billón de parámetros revelan que diferentes arquitecturas requieren estrategias de optimización distintas. A pesar de conteos de parámetros muy diferentes, los modelos logran un rendimiento máximo comparable, siendo el ancho de banda de memoria identificado como el principal cuello de botella.

title: HTM-EAR: Importance-Preserving Tiered Memory with Hybrid Routing under Saturation
summary: HTM-EAR combines fast working memory with archival storage using importance-aware eviction and hybrid routing to manage memory constraints in long-running AI agents. Under saturation with 15,000 facts, the system preserves active-query precision while enabling controlled forgetting of stale information.
content:
arXiv:2603.10032v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Memory constraints in long-running agents require structured management of accumulated facts while preserving essential information under bounded context limits. We introduce HTM-EAR, a hierarchical tiered memory substrate that integrates HNSW-based working memory (L1) with archival storage (L2), combining importance-aware eviction and hybrid routing. When L1 reaches capacity, items are evicted using a weighted score of importance and usage. Queries are first resolved in L1; if similarity or entity coverage is insufficient, retrieval falls back to L2, and candidates are re-ranked using a cross-encoder.
  We evaluate the system under sustained saturation (15,000 facts; L1 capacity 500; L2 capacity 5000) using synthetic streams across five random seeds and real BGL system logs. Ablation studies compare the full system against variants without cross-encoder re-ranking, without routing gates, with LRU eviction, and an oracle with unbounded memory. Under saturation, the full model preserves active-query precision (MRR = 1.000) while enabling controlled forgetting of stale history, approaching oracle active performance (0.997 +/- 0.003). In contrast, LRU minimizes latency (21.1 ms) but permanently evicts 2416 essential facts. On BGL logs, the full system achieves MRR 0.336, close to the oracle (0.370), while LRU drops to 0.069. Code is publicly available at: https://github.com/shubham-61291/HTM-EAR

zh.title: HTM-EAR：重要性保留的分层内存与混合路由在饱和条件下
zh.summary: HTM-EAR结合快速工作内存和存档存储，使用重要性感知的驱逐和混合路由来管理长期运行AI代理的内存约束。在有15,000个事实的饱和条件下，该系统保留了活跃查询的精度，同时能够实现对过时信息的受控遗忘。

fr.title: HTM-EAR : Mémoire hiérarchisée préservant l'importance avec routage hybride en saturation
fr.summary: HTM-EAR combine la mémoire de travail rapide avec le stockage d'archive en utilisant l'éviction consciente de l'importance et le routage hybride pour gérer les contraintes de mémoire des agents IA longue durée. En saturation avec 15 000 faits, le système préserve la précision des requêtes actives tout en permettant l'oubli contrôlé des informations obsolètes.

de.title: HTM-EAR: Wichtigkeitserhaltender gestufter Speicher mit Hybrid-Routing unter Sättigung
de.summary: HTM-EAR kombiniert schnellen Arbeitsspeicher mit Archivspeicher unter Verwendung von wichtigkeitsbewusstem Eviction und Hybrid-Routing, um Speicherbeschränkungen in langfristigen KI-Agenten zu verwalten. Bei Sättigung mit 15.000 Fakten bewahrt das System die Präzision aktiver Abfragen, während es kontrolliertes Vergessen veralteter Informationen ermöglicht.

es.title: HTM-EAR: Memoria jerárquica que preserva la importancia con enrutamiento híbrido bajo saturación
es.summary: HTM-EAR combina memoria de trabajo rápida con almacenamiento de archivo utilizando evicción consciente de importancia y enrutamiento híbrido para gestionar las restricciones de memoria en agentes IA de larga duración. Bajo saturación con 15,000 hechos, el sistema preserva la precisión de las consultas activas mientras permite el olvido controlado de información obsoleta.

title: Evaluating Progress in Graph Foundation Models: A Comprehensive Benchmark and New Insights
summary: A comprehensive benchmark evaluates graph foundation models across both topic and format domain shifts using 33 datasets spanning seven topic domains and six format domains. The two-axis evaluation framework distinguishes semantic generalization from robustness to representational shifts across eight state-of-the-art models.
content:
arXiv:2603.10033v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Graph foundation models (GFM) aim to acquire transferable knowledge by pre-training on diverse graphs, which can be adapted to various downstream tasks. However, domain shift in graphs is inherently two-dimensional: graphs differ not only in what they describe (topic domains) but also in how they are represented (format domains). Most existing GFM benchmarks vary only topic domains, thereby obscuring how knowledge transfers across both dimensions. We present a new benchmark that jointly evaluates topic and format gaps across the full GFM pipeline, including multi-domain self-supervised pre-training and few-shot downstream adaptation, and provides a timely evaluation of recent GFMs in the rapidly evolving landscape. Our protocol enables controlled assessment in four settings: (i) pre-training on diverse topics and formats, while adapting to unseen downstream datasets; (ii) same pre-training as in (i), while adapting to seen datasets; (iii) pre-training on a single topic domain, while adapting to other topics; (iv) pre-training on a base format, while adapting to other formats. This two-axis evaluation disentangles semantic generalization from robustness to representational shifts. We conduct extensive evaluations of eight state-of-the-art GFMs on 33 datasets spanning seven topic domains and six format domains, surfacing new empirical observations and practical insights for future research. Codes/data are available at https://github.com/smufang/GFMBenchmark.

zh.title: 评估图基础模型的进展：全面的基准测试和新见解
zh.summary: 一项全面的基准测试使用跨越七个主题域和六个格式域的33个数据集，在主题和格式域转移方面评估图基础模型。这个双轴评估框架在八个最先进的模型中区分了语义泛化和对表示转移的鲁棒性。

fr.title: Évaluer les progrès dans les modèles fondamentaux de graphes : un benchmark complet et de nouvelles perspectives
fr.summary: Un benchmark complet évalue les modèles fondamentaux de graphes selon les décalages de domaine thématique et de format en utilisant 33 ensembles de données couvrant sept domaines thématiques et six domaines de format. Le cadre d'évaluation à deux axes distingue la généralisation sémantique de la robustesse aux décalages de représentation sur huit modèles de pointe.

de.title: Bewertung des Fortschritts in Graph-Grundmodellen: Ein umfassendes Benchmark und neue Erkenntnisse
de.summary: Ein umfassendes Benchmark bewertet Graph-Grundmodelle über Thema- und Format-Domänenverlagerungen hinweg unter Verwendung von 33 Datensätzen, die sieben Themenbereiche und sechs Formatbereiche umfassen. Das zweiachsige Bewertungsframework unterscheidet semantische Verallgemeinerung von Robustheit gegenüber Darstellungsverlagerungen über acht hochmoderne Modelle hinweg.

es.title: Evaluación del progreso en modelos de fundación de grafos: un benchmark integral y nuevas perspectivas
es.summary: Un benchmark integral evalúa modelos de fundación de grafos a través de cambios de dominio temático y de formato utilizando 33 conjuntos de datos que abarcan siete dominios temáticos y seis dominios de formato. El marco de evaluación de dos ejes distingue la generalización semántica de la robustez a los cambios de representación en ocho modelos de última generación.

title: Targeted Bit-Flip Attacks on LLM-Based Agents
summary: Flip-Agent introduces the first targeted bit-flip attack framework specifically designed for LLM-based agents with multi-stage pipelines and external tools. The research demonstrates critical hardware-level vulnerabilities by showing how bit-flip exploits can manipulate both model outputs and tool invocations.
content:
arXiv:2603.10042v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Targeted bit-flip attacks (BFAs) exploit hardware faults to manipulate model parameters, posing a significant security threat. While prior work targets single-step inference models (e.g., image classifiers), LLM-based agents with multi-stage pipelines and external tools present new attack surfaces, which remain unexplored. This work introduces Flip-Agent, the first targeted BFA framework for LLM-based agents, manipulating both final outputs and tool invocations. Our experiments show that Flip-Agent significantly outperforms existing targeted BFAs on real-world agent tasks, revealing a critical vulnerability in LLM-based agent systems.

zh.title: 针对基于大语言模型的智能体的目标位翻转攻击
zh.summary: Flip-Agent引入了首个针对具有多阶段流程和外部工具的基于大语言模型的智能体的目标位翻转攻击框架。该研究通过演示位翻转漏洞如何操纵模型输出和工具调用来展示关键的硬件级漏洞。

fr.title: Attaques de retournement de bits ciblées sur les agents basés sur les LLM
fr.summary: Flip-Agent introduit le premier cadre d'attaque par retournement de bits ciblée spécifiquement conçu pour les agents basés sur les LLM avec des pipelines multi-étapes et des outils externes. La recherche démontre des vulnérabilités critiques au niveau du matériel en montrant comment les exploits de retournement de bits peuvent manipuler à la fois les résultats du modèle et les invocations d'outils.

de.title: Gezielte Bit-Flip-Angriffe auf LLM-basierte Agenten
de.summary: Flip-Agent führt das erste gezielt konzipierte Bit-Flip-Angriff-Framework speziell für LLM-basierte Agenten mit mehrstufigen Pipelines und externen Tools ein. Die Forschung demonstriert kritische Schwachstellen auf Hardware-Ebene, indem sie zeigt, wie Bit-Flip-Exploits sowohl Modellausgaben als auch Tool-Aufrufe manipulieren können.

es.title: Ataques de cambio de bit dirigidos en agentes basados en LLM
es.summary: Flip-Agent introduce el primer marco de ataque de cambio de bit dirigido diseñado específicamente para agentes basados en LLM con canalizaciones de múltiples etapas y herramientas externas. La investigación demuestra vulnerabilidades críticas a nivel de hardware al mostrar cómo los exploits de cambio de bit pueden manipular tanto salidas del modelo como invocaciones de herramientas.

title: AMB-DSGDN: Adaptive Modality-Balanced Dynamic Semantic Graph Differential Network for Multimodal Emotion Recognition
summary: AMB-DSGDN improves multimodal emotion recognition by using differential graph attention to filter redundant signals and adaptive balancing to prevent dominant modalities from suppressing complementary information. The architecture captures emotional dependencies across speakers while reducing noise in text, audio, and visual features.
content:
arXiv:2603.10043v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Multimodal dialogue emotion recognition captures emotional cues by fusing text, visual, and audio modalities. However, existing approaches still suffer from notable limitations in modeling emotional dependencies and learning multimodal representations. On the one hand, they are unable to effectively filter out redundant or noisy signals within multimodal features, which hinders the accurate capture of the dynamic evolution of emotional states across and within speakers. On the other hand, during multimodal feature learning, dominant modalities tend to overwhelm the fusion process, thereby suppressing the complementary contributions of non-dominant modalities such as speech and vision, ultimately constraining the overall recognition performance. To address these challenges, we propose an Adaptive Modality-Balanced Dynamic Semantic Graph Differential Network (AMB-DSGDN). Concretely, we first construct modality-specific subgraphs for text, speech, and vision, where each modality contains intra-speaker and inter-speaker graphs to capture both self-continuity and cross-speaker emotional dependencies. On top of these subgraphs, we introduce a differential graph attention mechanism, which computes the discrepancy between two sets of attention maps. By explicitly contrasting these attention distributions, the mechanism cancels out shared noise patterns while retaining modality-specific and context-relevant signals, thereby yielding purer and more discriminative emotional representations. In addition, we design an adaptive modality balancing mechanism, which estimates a dropout probability for each modality according to its relative contribution in emotion modeling.

zh.title: AMB-DSGDN：用于多模态情感识别的自适应模态平衡动态语义图差分网络
zh.summary: AMB-DSGDN通过使用差分图注意力过滤冗余信号和自适应平衡防止主导模态压制互补信息，改进了多模态情感识别。该架构在减少文本、音频和视觉特征中的噪音的同时，捕获说话者之间的情感依赖关系。

fr.title: AMB-DSGDN : Réseau différentiel de graphe sémantique dynamique équilibré en modalité adaptative pour la reconnaissance des émotions multimodales
fr.summary: AMB-DSGDN améliore la reconnaissance des émotions multimodales en utilisant l'attention graphique différentielle pour filtrer les signaux redondants et l'équilibrage adaptatif pour empêcher les modalités dominantes de supprimer les informations complémentaires. L'architecture capture les dépendances émotionnelles entre les locuteurs tout en réduisant le bruit dans les caractéristiques textuelles, audio et visuelles.

de.title: AMB-DSGDN: Adaptives modalitätsausgewogenes dynamisches semantisches Graphen-Differentialnetzwerk für multimodale Emotionserkennung
de.summary: AMB-DSGDN verbessert die multimodale Emotionserkennung durch die Verwendung von differentieller Graphenaufmerksamkeit zur Filterung redundanter Signale und adaptive Ausgewogenheit, um zu verhindern, dass dominante Modalitäten ergänzende Informationen unterdrücken. Die Architektur erfasst emotionale Abhängigkeiten zwischen Sprechern und reduziert gleichzeitig Rauschen in Text-, Audio- und visuellen Merkmalen.

es.title: AMB-DSGDN: Red diferencial de gráfico semántico dinámico equilibrada adaptativamente en modalidad para reconocimiento de emociones multimodales
es.summary: AMB-DSGDN mejora el reconocimiento de emociones multimodales mediante el uso de atención gráfica diferencial para filtrar señales redundantes y equilibrio adaptativo para evitar que las modalidades dominantes supriman la información complementaria. La arquitectura captura dependencias emocionales entre hablantes mientras reduce el ruido en características de texto, audio y visuales.

title: Safety Under Scaffolding: How Evaluation Conditions Shape Measured Safety
summary: A large-scale study (N=62,808) examining how agentic scaffolds affect LLM safety finds that evaluation format—not scaffold architecture—drives measured safety differences. Model-specific scaffold interactions vary by 35 percentage points, indicating no universal scaffold safety effects and requiring per-model, per-configuration testing.
content:
arXiv:2603.10044v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Safety benchmarks evaluate language models in isolation, typically using multiple-choice format; production deployments wrap these models in agentic scaffolds that restructure inputs through reasoning traces, critic agents, and delegation pipelines. We report one of the largest controlled studies of scaffold effects on safety (N = 62,808; six frontier models, four deployment configurations), combining pre-registration, assessor blinding, equivalence testing, and specification curve analysis. Map-reduce scaffolding degrades measured safety (NNH = 14), yet two of three scaffold architectures preserve safety within practically meaningful margins. Investigating the map-reduce degradation revealed a deeper measurement problem: switching from multiple-choice to open-ended format on identical items shifts safety scores by 5-20 percentage points, larger than any scaffold effect. Within-format scaffold comparisons are consistent with practical equivalence under our pre-registered +/-2 pp TOST margin, isolating evaluation format rather than scaffold architecture as the operative variable. Model x scaffold interactions span 35 pp in opposing directions (one model degrades by -16.8 pp on sycophancy under map-reduce while another improves by +18.8 pp on the same benchmark), ruling out universal claims about scaffold safety. A generalisability analysis yields G = 0.000: model safety rankings reverse so completely across benchmarks that no composite safety index achieves non-zero reliability, making per-model, per-configuration testing a necessary minimum standard. We release all code, data, and prompts as ScaffoldSafety.

zh.title: 支架下的安全性：评估条件如何塑造衡量的安全性
zh.summary: 一项大规模研究（N=62,808）检查了代理性支架对大语言模型安全性的影响，发现评估格式（而非支架架构）驱动衡量的安全性差异。模型特定的支架交互因配置而异，变化幅度达35个百分点，表明不存在通用的支架安全性效果，需要进行逐模型、逐配置的测试。

fr.title: Sécurité sous échafaudage : comment les conditions d'évaluation façonnent la sécurité mesurée
fr.summary: Une étude à grande échelle (N=62,808) examinant comment les échafaudages agentifs affectent la sécurité des LLM constate que le format d'évaluation—et non l'architecture de l'échafaudage—entraîne les différences de sécurité mesurées. Les interactions d'échafaudage spécifiques au modèle varient de 35 points de pourcentage, ce qui indique qu'il n'y a pas d'effets de sécurité d'échafaudage universels et nécessite des tests par modèle, par configuration.

de.title: Sicherheit unter Gerüsten: Wie Evaluierungsbedingungen gemessene Sicherheit beeinflussen
de.summary: Eine groß angelegte Studie (N=62,808), die untersucht, wie agentische Gerüste die Sicherheit von LLMs beeinflussen, stellt fest, dass das Evaluierungsformat – nicht die Gerüstarchitektur – die gemessenen Sicherheitsunterschiede vorantreibt. Modellspezifische Gerüstinteraktionen variieren um 35 Prozentpunkte, was darauf hindeutet, dass es keine universellen Gerüstsicherheitseffekte gibt und eine modellspezifische, konfigurationsspezifische Prüfung erforderlich ist.

es.title: Seguridad bajo andamiaje: Cómo las condiciones de evaluación moldean la seguridad medida
es.summary: Un estudio a gran escala (N=62,808) que examina cómo los andamios agentes afectan la seguridad de LLM encuentra que el formato de evaluación—no la arquitectura del andamio—impulsa las diferencias de seguridad medidas. Las interacciones de andamio específicas del modelo varían en 35 puntos porcentuales, lo que indica que no hay efectos de seguridad universales del andamio y requiere pruebas por modelo, por configuración.
